mapping.a_element: transition between a passive phase and an active phase with spontaneous flow
mapping.b_element: phase transition from a disordered to a flocking state

mapping.a_element: activity parameter (extensile/contractile rods) driving the transition
mapping.b_element: particle speed and alignment interactions driving the flocking transition

mapping.a_element: hydrodynamic equations governing momentum and order parameter dynamics
mapping.b_element: macroscopic transport equations derived from kinetic theory

mapping.a_element: lattice Boltzmann method for momentum transport
mapping.b_element: Enskog-type kinetic theory for coarse-graining microscopic dynamics

brief_justification: While both papers study phase transitions in active systems and derive hydrodynamic-like equations, the underlying mechanisms differ: Paper A uses a hybrid lattice Boltzmann approach for active nematics with anchoring conditions and order parameter dynamics, whereas Paper B employs kinetic theory to coarse-grain the Vicsek model's microscopic flocking dynamics. The core machinery (LB vs. kinetic theory) and the nature of the transitions (spontaneous flow vs. alignment-driven flocking) are distinct.
